title: Holographic reconstruction from entanglement entropy, Wilson loops, and geodesic constraints
bridge_note: Bulk reconstruction from EE, Wilson loops, and geodesics demonstrates that a single observable is insufficient; multiple independent probes reduce degeneracy but require sufficient independence to uniquely determine geometry.
cosine_bridge: EE ⊕ WL ⊕ GEO act as constraint ensemble. Individual probes leave degeneracy; combined, orthogonal constraints phase-lock reconstruction (45° 📐) → identifiability emerges when constraints are independent.
engagement_detail: Tweet + Grok framing mapped EE ⊕ WL ⊕ GEO to constraint ensemble and phase-lock reconstruction (45° gate).
notes_internal: Direct correspondence to btz-phase-lock repo: EE-only underdetermined; EE⊕WL resolves; EE⊕WL⊕GEO yields unique reconstruction (orthogonality threshold).
